---
title: "Changelog"
output: html_document
---

## v2.3
**2026-03-25**

**Public version added**, minor code imrovements done.
The source code of the IsoQC app is available on GitHub at the following link: 
<a href="https://github.com/erdelyidani/IsoQC" target="_blank">
  IsoQC GitHub repository
</a>


## v2.2.3
**2026-03-12**

**Fixed:** Date-range updates now commit correctly from both the slider and manual *From* / *To* inputs.
- Reworked the slider handling so the dashboard uses a dedicated commit event from the date slider instead of the live drag state.
- Manual *From* / *To* edits now immediately update the committed dashboard interval and refresh the charts again.
- Station-specific date bounds and the *Reset date range* action continue to stay synchronized with both input modes.

**Fixed:** Basemap switching now preserves the current map state.
- Switching between *Light* and *Terrain* no longer recreates the leaflet widget.
- The selected station, nearby stations, distance lines and current map focus now remain visible after a basemap change.


## v2.2.2
**2026-03-12**

**Fixed:** Date-range slider no longer commits heavy dashboard recalculations while it is still being dragged.
- Preview updates coming from the live slider are now isolated from the committed dashboard date interval.
- Nearby-station, distance-based and date-filtered calculations now wait for the final slider release event before the new period is applied.
- The linked *From* / *To* controls continue to support direct manual date edits and reset behaviour.

**Added:** Selectable map basemap on the Dashboard.
- Added a *Basemap* selector directly on the map.
- The map can now switch between the existing light basemap and a terrain/topographic basemap.


## v2.2.1
**2026-03-12**

**Enhanced:** Date-range slider now commits only after release.
- Dashboard recalculations that depend on the selected date range now use a committed date interval instead of the live slider position.
- While the date-range slider is being dragged, only the visible *From* / *To* controls are updated; nearby-station and distance-based calculations wait until the mouse button or touch interaction is released.
- Manual *From* / *To* date edits and the *Reset date range* button still apply immediately.


## v2.2.0
**2026-03-12**

**Added:** Station-specific date range controller on the Dashboard.
- Replaced the previous minimum/maximum date filtering workflow with a visible date-range slider.
- Added manual *From* / *To* date pickers above the slider for direct start/end selection.
- Enabled dragging the selected interval on the slider while preserving the chosen window length.
- Added a *Reset date range* button that restores the full available period of the selected station.
- The slider bounds now always follow the selected station and are defined from the earliest and latest dates with available δ¹⁸O and/or δ²H measurements, ensuring both isotope series are considered.

**Enhanced:** Faster nearby-station distance handling.
- Reworked distance calculations to use station-to-all distances only for the currently selected station instead of rebuilding a full station-by-station distance matrix.
- Updated the nearby-station map highlighting and neighbour selection logic to reuse the faster distance results.


## v2.1.0
**2025-12-03**
 
**Added:** Built-in usage logging.
- Added server-side SQLite usage logging for session starts and ends, tab views, data import/test-dataset loading, exports and changelog opens.
- Usage metrics are stored locally in the app log database to support lightweight adoption analytics and internal monitoring, see more in the Instructions tab.


## v2.0.9
**2025-11-19**
 
**Enhanced:** Exported data using the *“Download mean time series”* button (previously "Download plot data (.csv)") is limited to nearby mean values


## v2.0.8
**2025-11-18**

**Added: Export time-series data feature.**  
**Enhanced: Differences bars coloring rules.**  
- New *“Download plot data (.csv)”* button under the map exports the currently displayed time-series data for δ¹⁸O, δ²H and D-excess.  
- The CSV includes, for each date and variable, the selected station, the nearby mean, the |site − mean| differences, and the active STD×2.  
- Each row is accompanied by the analysis context: nearby search radius (km), isotope-specific thresholds, elevation correction slopes for δ¹⁸O and δ²H, and the active date range (*From* / *To*), ensuring that exported diagnostics are fully traceable to the dashboard settings.


## v2.0.7
**2025-11-14**

**Enhanced:** Elevation correction is now station-centric.
- Altitude reference for the elevation correction was changed from the dataset-wide minimum altitude to the altitude of the currently selected station, making corrected δ¹⁸O and δ²H values directly comparable to the local setting.

**Added: STD×2-based bar colouring.**  
- Difference bars in the δ¹⁸O, δ²H and D-excess time-series are now coloured using both the threshold and the STD×2 metric:  
  - Grey: difference ≤ threshold and STD×2 metric ≤ difference.  
  - Red: difference ≤ threshold but STD×2 metric > difference (potentially suspicious, but still under threshold).  
  - Purple with red outline: difference above the configured threshold.  
- Hover tooltips on difference bars now report both the difference and the STD×2 metric.


## v2.0.6
**2025-09-09**

**Enhanced:** Improved missing-data handling and user experience in the Dashboard:  
- Time-series plots (δ¹⁸O, δ²H) now remain blank instead of failing when data are unavailable for the selected station and date range.  
- D‑excess and XY plots also remain blank when no paired isotope data exist.  
- Introduced a unified warning popup system that displays a single message when one or both isotopes are missing, skips the popup during initial data load, and prevents conflicts with the changelog popup message.


## v2.0.5
**2025-07-16**

**Fixed:** Preview table now shows dates in dd/mm/YYYY format instead of Excel serial numbers.
**Enhanced:** Station Data Availability plot uses the full dataset and offers dynamic hover tooltips (daily: DD/MM/YYYY, monthly: MM/YYYY, yearly: YYYY).


## v2.0.4
**2025-06-20**

**Added:** Interactive map tab with leaflet-based station visualization and customizable radius filtering.


## v2.0.3
**2025-05-10**

**Enhanced:** Data import wizard now fully supports both Excel and CSV inputs with custom delimiters and date‐format parsing.


## v2.0.2
**2025-04-01**

**Improved:** Time-series plots gained threshold‐bar overlays and automated mean‐comparison lines for robust quality checks.


## v2.0.1
**2025-02-15**

**Initial 2.0 release:** Redesigned UI with bslib theming and introduced core isotope validation dashboards and summary metrics.
